title: Amendment to SCT Rates and Tax Bases in List II for Passenger Cars
bodyHtml:
The SCT (Special Consumption Tax) rates and corresponding tax bases applicable to passenger cars under tariff heading 8703 have been revised. The changes entered into force today.

For vehicles with an engine displacement not exceeding 1600 cm³, the SCT rates, which varied between 45% and 80% depending on the tax base, have been changed as follows:

-- For vehicles with an engine displacement not exceeding 1400 cm³, the rates ranging from 70% to 90% based on the tax base

-- For vehicles with an engine displacement exceeding 1400 cm³ but not exceeding 1600 cm³, the rates ranging from 75% to 100% based on the tax base

For vehicles with an engine displacement exceeding 1600 cm³ but not exceeding 2000 cm³:

-- For those equipped with an electric motor, where the electric motor power exceeds 50 kW and the engine displacement does not exceed 1800 cm³, the SCT rates, previously ranging from 45% to 80%, have been changed to 70% to 80%.

-- For other vehicles, the SCT rates previously ranging from 130% to 150% have been updated to 150% to 170%.

For vehicles with an engine displacement exceeding 2000 cm³:

-- For those equipped with an electric motor, where the electric motor power exceeds 100 kW and the engine displacement does not exceed 2500 cm³, the rates have been changed from 130%–150% to 150%–170%.

-- For other vehicles, the 220% SCT rate remains unchanged.

For plug-in hybrid vehicles (externally chargeable) equipped with an electric motor, if their weighted average carbon dioxide emission is below 25 grams per kilometer and their electric-only range is 70 kilometers or more, the rates previously ranging from 30% to 70% have been changed to 45% to 85%.

For fully electric vehicles, the rates, which previously ranged from 10% to 60%, have been updated to range from 25% to 75%.

Click here for the decision and the updated rate-base table.
